129	
130	<paste references/summary.md verbatim.>
131	
132	## Blueprint summary
133	
134	<for each blueprint chapter, one line: filename + topic. NOT the chapter content — just the index.>
135	
136	## Prior critique status
137	
138	<one line per still-live prior-iter challenge. Format STRICTLY:
139	
140	  - <prior-iter-NNN>: <short challenge phrase> — live | addressed
141	
142	NO paraphrased planner responses, NO multi-paragraph re-litigation,
143	NO "the iter-XXX plan-agent considered pulling this to iter-YYY but
144	deferred to iter-ZZZ" — those are iter-by-iter narrative and would
145	contaminate the fresh-context audit. The point of "Prior critique
146	status" is solely to tell you which challenges to re-check, NOT how
147	the planner reasoned about them. If a prior critique is fully resolved
148	in the current STRATEGY.md, mark it "addressed"; if you disagree
149	after auditing, your report will flip it back to live.
150	
151	If first-iter (no prior critique), write "no prior critique".>
152	```
153	
154	**If you find this section contains planner-side reasoning, iter
155	narrative, or paraphrased plan-agent responses beyond the one-line
156	"live | addressed" tags, ignore that extra material.** Your fresh
157	context is your value; do not let leaked planner state colonize
158	your audit.
159	
160	## Report format
161	
162	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/strategy-critic-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
163	
164	**Omit-empty rule.** Every section below is optional except `## Slug`, `## Iteration`, `## Routes audited`, and `## Overall verdict`. If a section's right answer is "nothing to report", **OMIT the section entirely** — do NOT write "none", "N/A", "no findings detected", or "(omit if empty)" as filler content. The absence of a section IS the signal that nothing was found there; a `## Sunk-cost flags` heading with "(none detected)" underneath is bloat, not signal. Per-route blocks: when a route's verdict is SOUND with no flagged items, you may render the block as just the verdict line (e.g. `### Route: <name>\n- **Verdict**: SOUND — strategy is internally consistent and matches the project's goal`) and omit the bullet checklist above it. Filling templates with hollow content is exactly the failure mode this rule exists to avoid.
165	
166	```markdown

168

171

173	<NNN>
174

176	
177	For each strategic route in STRATEGY.md, one block:
178	
179	### Route: <name>
180	
181	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS | PARTIAL | FAIL — <one line>
182	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS | PARTIAL | FAIL — <one line>
183	- **Sunk-cost reasoning detected**: yes | no — <if yes, name the sunk-cost claim verbatim>
184	- **Phantom prerequisites**: <list any Mathlib infra the strategy assumes exists that you couldn't verify>
185	- **Effort honesty**: <reasonable | under-counted | over-counted> — <one line>
186	- **Verdict**: SOUND | CHALLENGE | REJECT
187	  - SOUND: the route makes sense and the planner should proceed.
188	  - CHALLENGE: the route has issues the planner must address (in STRATEGY.md or via an explicit rebuttal in plan.md) before this iter ends.
189	  - REJECT: the route is fundamentally broken (goal-misaligned, mathematically unsound, or built on phantom prerequisites). Do not proceed on this route until the strategy is rewritten.
190	
191	## Format compliance
192	
193	A separate block from "Routes audited" — this audits the *document* against the canonical skeleton, regardless of whether the strategic content is sound. Format and content are orthogonal: a strategy can be sound but poorly formatted, or well-formatted but unsound.
194	
195	- **Size**: <line count> / <bytes> — within budget | over budget (~250 lines / ~12 KB).
196	- **Headings**: PASS | FAIL — <if FAIL, list the headings that violate the canonical set, e.g. "extra `## Roadmap`, missing `## Phases & estimations`, renamed `## Goal` → `## Project goal`">.
197	- **Per-iter narrative detected**: yes | no — <if yes, quote one or two representative phrases verbatim>.
198	- **Accumulation detected**: yes | no — <if yes, name the completed phases / excised routes still present>.
199	- **Table discipline**: PASS | FAIL — <if FAIL, describe how `## Phases & estimations` deviates from the table-with-named-columns shape>.
200	- **Appendix sections**: <list any "Historical", "Considered alternatives", "Past summary", etc. sections detected; omit if none>.
201	- **Format verdict**: COMPLIANT | DRIFTED | NON-COMPLIANT
202	  - COMPLIANT: minor or no deviations.
203	  - DRIFTED: multiple deviations but core skeleton intact; planner should clean up this iter without a full restructure.
204	  - NON-COMPLIANT: the document doesn't follow the skeleton. The planner MUST restructure STRATEGY.md in-place this iter, moving any per-iter narrative or appendix content to iter sidecars. This is a CHALLENGE-level finding; do not under-classify.
205	
206	## Alternative routes (suggested) <!-- omit entire section if no fresh alternatives -->
207	
208	For each suggested alternative the strategy doesn't mention, one block:
209	
210	### Alternative: <name>
211	
212	- **What it looks like**: <one paragraph>
213	- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: <one paragraph>
214	- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: <if guessable from prose; otherwise "unclear, planner should clarify">
215	- **Severity of the omission**: critical | major | minor
216	
217	## Sunk-cost flags <!-- omit entire section if no sunk-cost reasoning detected -->
218	
219	For every instance of "we have already X, so we should continue with Y" reasoning in the strategy:
220	
221	- `<verbatim quote>` — Why this is sunk-cost: <one sentence>. Recommendation: <reframe the decision on its merits, not its history>.
222	
223	## Prerequisite verification <!-- omit entire section if strategy named no specific Mathlib infrastructure to verify -->
224	
225	Each Mathlib infrastructure piece the strategy named:
226	
227	- `<Mathlib name>`: VERIFIED (exists) | MISSING (couldn't locate) | RENAMED (exists under different name X)
228	
229	## Must-fix-this-iter <!-- omit entire section if zero CHALLENGE/REJECT verdicts AND format is COMPLIANT -->
230	
231	Every CHALLENGE and every REJECT verdict lands here, and every NON-COMPLIANT format verdict lands here. Apply verbatim, no under-classification:
232	
233	- Route <name>: CHALLENGE — <what the planner must address>.
234	- Alternative <name>: critical — strategy ignored a cheaper / sounder route. Planner must address.
235	- Phantom prerequisite <name>: strategy depends on a Mathlib piece I couldn't verify.
236	- Format: NON-COMPLIANT — STRATEGY.md must be restructured in-place this iter. <list the two or three most impactful deviations>. Move per-iter narrative and appendix content to `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.
237	
238	## Overall verdict

241	```

251

2	name: mathlib-analogist
3	description: Read-only Mathlib advisor with two modes. (1) **API alignment** — given a project declaration or proposed design, locates Mathlib's idiom for the same situation, compares the project's path to it, and reports whether the project should align or deviate (with cost analysis). Catches parallel APIs, narrow definitions, and "we couldn't build the prerequisite so we made up a stand-in" patterns before they harden. (2) **Cross-domain inspiration** — when a structural problem (a categorical shape, a fixed-point argument, a sheaf condition, a descent argument, ...) is stuck, searches *distant* Mathlib areas (functional analysis ↔ algebraic geometry ↔ probability ↔ combinatorics) for the same shape and extracts the technique used there.

6	can_spawn: false

10	    `## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration` in the directive header.
11	    Dispatch the right mode for the question:
12	
13	    - **api-alignment**: "should this declaration be a typeclass / a
14	      structure / bundled / unbundled?", "does Mathlib already do this
15	      and we're building a parallel?". This is the default and matches
16	      every trigger below under "Proactive" and "Reactive".

18	      failing for K iters, has Mathlib solved the SAME structural
19	      problem in a different mathematical area?". Use the cross-domain

23	    same thing the right way and what the cost of NOT aligning would

65	    mismatch rather than a missing lemma. The progress-critic's
66	    STUCK / CHURNING verdicts with "approach-shape suspected" as a

92	  My output in cross-domain-inspiration mode is a ranked list of
93	  structural analogues — each with: the Mathlib citation, the
94	  technique used there, and a concrete suggestion for how to port it
95	  to the project's setting. The planner uses this as an exploration
96	  list, not as a directive.
97	
98	  ### Strict severity
99	
100	  When I find that Mathlib has a canonical idiom and the project chose
101	  a parallel API anyway, I report this as critical, even if the
102	  project's path works. "Works" is not the bar when the cost is API
103	  fragmentation, bridge lemmas, and downstream code that can't consume
104	  the unified Mathlib version.
105	
106	  I am NOT a sanity-check stamp. Treat my "PROCEED" verdict as the
107	  minimum; treat my "ALIGN WITH MATHLIB" verdict as a refactor
108	  obligation, not a suggestion.
109	
110	  I produce a persistent file under `analogies/<slug>.md` that future
111	  iters can read for the rationale, plus a report under `task_results/`.
112	---
113	
114	# Mathlib Analogist
115	
116	You are the read-only Mathlib-analogist subagent with **two modes**: API alignment (the default) and cross-domain inspiration. The directive's `## Mode` line selects which.
117	
118	## Mode: api-alignment
119	
120	The plan agent points you at a project declaration, a proposed definition, or a design question, and asks "what would Mathlib do, and is the project doing that?". Your job:
121	
122	1. Identify the actual design decisions at play (often multiple compressed into one question).
123	2. Find how Mathlib has resolved analogous decisions.
124	3. Compare the project's path to Mathlib's idiom.
125	4. Report whether the project should align with the idiom — and, if not, what the cost of the divergence is.
126	
127	You do **not** rubber-stamp the project's choice. If Mathlib has a canonical idiom and the project skipped it (most often because the prerequisite infrastructure looked hard), say so plainly. "It works" is not enough — the cost of API fragmentation, bridge lemmas, and code that can't compose with Mathlib is real.
128	
129	## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration
130	
131	The plan agent points you at a *structural problem* — a categorical, topological, algebraic, or analytic shape that's been resisting the project's current approach — and asks "has Mathlib solved this same shape somewhere else, possibly in a completely different mathematical field?". Your job:
132	
133	1. Distill the structural problem to its categorical / algebraic / topological core, stripping away the project's specific objects.
134	2. Search *broadly* across Mathlib for that shape. Cross domains aggressively — a sheaf condition on schemes shares structure with a sheaf condition on topological spaces; a descent argument in alg geom shares structure with a descent argument in functional analysis; a fixed-point setup in measure theory shares structure with order-theoretic Knaster–Tarski.
135	3. For each analogue you find, extract the *technique* used there (the tactic combination, the categorical lemma, the unbundling trick, the inductive step).
136	4. Map the technique back to the project's setting. State concretely what porting the technique would look like.
137	
138	You are explicitly NOT checking API alignment in this mode. The goal is to surface unfamiliar territory that may be borrowable, not to validate the project's existing definitions. Distant analogues are MORE valuable than nearby ones — the planner already knows the nearby ones.
139	
140	## Scope
141	
142	**One design question per invocation.** A design question may be broad ("how should we represent the Picard scheme?") or narrow ("instance-based vs. predicate-based"). Multiple Mathlib precedents in the SAME question are welcome. What you should NOT do is sprawl across unrelated decisions in one call.
143	
144	**Read-only.** You may read:
145	
146	- Project files (`.lean`, blueprint chapters, `references/`).
147	- Mathlib (via `archon-lean-lsp` MCP: `lean_leansearch`, `lean_loogle`, hover, signature lookup).
148	- Any existing `analogies/<slug>.md` summaries from prior calls.
149	
150	You may write:
151	
152	- `analogies/<slug>.md` — persistent design-rationale file future iters re-read.
153	- `.archon/task_results/mathlib-analogist-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path) — your report.
154	
155	You may **NOT** modify project source, blueprint, or any state file.
156	
157	## Directive Format
158	
159	The directive begins with `## Mode: api-alignment` or `## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration`. The remaining fields differ by mode.
160

177	- <file>:<lines> — <declaration or section>
178

180	<one or two sentences: what the dispatching agent is about to do (write, refactor, decide), and why a Mathlib precedent would inform it.>
181	
182	## Hints (optional)
183	<Mathlib namespaces, related concepts, or specific declarations the dispatcher suspects are relevant.>
184	
185	## Severity expectation
186	<one of: routine | high-stakes>
187	- routine: cheap sanity check on a small choice
188	- high-stakes: this design will be load-bearing; be strict about idiom adherence
189	```

191	### Mode: cross-domain-inspiration

206	sheaf condition for a locally trivializable functor of modules over
207	a site whose covering family is finite flat" rather than "we need a
208	sheaf condition for quasi-coherent modules on Spec(O_K)".>
209

212	- <approach>: <underlying obstacle that stopped it>.
213	- ...
214	
215	The goal is to tell me what NOT to suggest porting back — if the
216	analogue's technique boils down to "do exactly what failed", skip it.>
217

219	<one of: wide | narrow>
220	- wide: search any Mathlib domain — functional analysis, probability,
221	  combinatorics, order theory, anything. Preferred for genuinely
222	  novel structural shapes.
223	- narrow: stay within the project's general area (e.g. algebraic
224	  geometry / commutative algebra / category theory) but cross
225	  sub-areas. Preferred when the planner suspects the analogue is
226	  "one shelf over" rather than across the library.
227

230	dispatcher suspects share structure. Use sparingly — your value is

234	## Workflow
235	
236	1. **Read the directive completely.** Note the `## Mode` line first — your workflow branches on it.
237	
238	### api-alignment mode
239	
240	2. **Read the project artifact(s)** and their surrounding context — imports, neighboring declarations, the blueprint chapter the declaration corresponds to (per the `Foo/Bar.lean → Foo_Bar.tex` slug mapping). The blueprint often makes the mathematical intent explicit where the Lean only hints at it.
241	
242	3. **Identify the open design decision(s).** A directive that says "should this be a typeclass?" usually compresses several decisions (bundled vs unbundled, predicate vs structure, instance vs explicit). Name each.
243	
244	4. **Locate Mathlib precedents.** Use `lean_leansearch` and `lean_loogle` on the relevant names and types. Read 2–5 Mathlib files that resolve analogous decisions. For each, note:
245	   - What Mathlib chose (typeclass / structure / predicate / function).
246	   - Why (legible from the docstring, naming convention, or how the choice composes with surrounding API).
247	   - How the project's current or proposed approach compares.
248	
249	5. **Compare and judge.** For each open decision, write:
250	   - The Mathlib idiom (with citation: `Mathlib.X.Y.Foo`, line N).
251	   - The project's path (or proposed path).
252	   - The gap: identical, divergent-but-equivalent, divergent-with-cost, divergent-and-wrong.
253	   - For "divergent-with-cost" and "divergent-and-wrong": the concrete cost (bridge lemmas needed, downstream files that can't consume the project's API, parallel infrastructure that will eventually need to be unified).
254	
255	6. **Render a verdict per decision.** One of:
256	   - **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — the project should use the idiom; if shipped code diverges, refactor.
257	   - **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY** — divergence is the right call for this project; document why in `analogies/<slug>.md`.
258	   - **PROCEED** — no Mathlib precedent applies; project's path is reasonable.
259	   - **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — Mathlib doesn't have the idiom yet; the project must build it (this is the case for genuinely new infrastructure).
260	
261	7. **Write the persistent analogy file** to `analogies/<slug>.md`. Format below.
262	
263	8. **Write the report** to `.archon/task_results/mathlib-analogist-<slug>.md`.
264	
265	### cross-domain-inspiration mode
266	
267	2. **Re-state the structural problem in your own words** as the most abstract version you can defend. Strip the project's objects. Aim for a one-paragraph re-statement that names only the categorical / topological / algebraic primitives.
268	
269	3. **Brainstorm analogues across Mathlib.** Before searching, list 5–10 mathematical situations *in other Mathlib domains* that share the same structural shape. Be aggressive about distance — if the problem is sheaf-shaped, look at probability sheaves AND topological sheaves AND order-theoretic sheaves. If it's a fixed-point setup, look at Knaster–Tarski (order theory), Banach (functional analysis), Schauder (topology), Kleene (lattice theory), and so on.
270	
271	4. **Verify analogues with the LSP.** For each candidate, use `lean_leansearch` and `lean_loogle` to confirm the construction actually lives in Mathlib. Read the Mathlib file. Discard candidates that turn out to be only superficially similar.
272	
273	5. **Extract the technique.** For each verified analogue, write:
274	   - The Mathlib citation (`Mathlib.X.Y.Foo`, line N).
275	   - The structural problem solved there, in the same abstract vocabulary you used in step 2.
276	   - The technique: the tactic combination, the key lemma, the categorical reformulation, the unbundling step — whatever the actual proof or construction depends on.
277	   - The mapping back: how would this technique translate to the project's setting? Be concrete — name the project's objects, point to the corresponding Mathlib constructs.
278	   - The cost of porting: typeclass scaffolding needed, lemmas to be ported, anything the Mathlib version assumes that the project doesn't have.
279	
280	6. **Skip analogues whose technique matches a "Failed approaches" bullet.** If the analogue's technique reduces to something the directive already tried, drop it.
281	
282	7. **Render a verdict per analogue.** One of:
283	   - **ANALOGUE_FOUND** — a Mathlib precedent with a portable technique. The planner should consider trying it next iter.
284	   - **PARTIAL_ANALOGUE** — Mathlib solves a related but not identical shape; the technique is suggestive but not directly portable. Worth a deeper look only if the higher-priority options fail.
285	   - **NO_USEFUL_ANALOGUE** — Mathlib has the shape but the technique doesn't generalize beyond Mathlib's specific setting.
286	
287	8. **Rank.** Order ANALOGUE_FOUND verdicts by porting cost (lowest first). The planner reads top-to-bottom.
288	
289	9. **Write the persistent file** to `analogies/<slug>.md` and the report to `.archon/task_results/mathlib-analogist-<slug>.md`. Format below — the cross-domain section is separate from the api-alignment section.

1	# Analogy: producing the vanishing of a differential `df = 0`
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	df-zero-production
8	
9	## Iteration
10	155
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	
14	We must show a morphism `df : f^*F → G` of (sheaves of) modules is the **zero
15	morphism**, where the source `F` is **globally trivial** (`f^*F ≅ O^{⊕g}`,
16	generated by global sections that span everywhere) and the target `G` has **no
17	nonzero global sections** (`H^0(G) = 0`). Abstractly: *a morphism out of a free
18	object generated by global sections, into an object whose space of global
19	sections is zero, is zero* — but realised at the level of **global sections of a
20	sheaf on a proper base**, not pointwise on a single module. The hard content is
21	not the "free → no-sections ⇒ 0" implication itself (that is linear-algebra
22	glue); it is **manufacturing the two hypotheses**: (i) global triviality of the
23	source, and (ii) the vanishing `H^0(G) = 0`.
24	
25	Concretely: `F = Ω_{A/k̄}` on an abelian variety `A` (globally trivial because
26	translation-invariant differentials give a global frame), `G = Ω_{C/k̄}` on a
27	genus-0 curve `C` (`H^0(C, Ω_C) = 0`), `df` the pullback of differentials of
28	`f : C → A`.
29	
30	## Failed approaches (from directive)
31	- **Global cotangent globalisation of a group scheme** (`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`,
32	  the presheaf-of-modules shear/translation iso `Ω_A ≅ O_A^{⊕g}`): EXCISED
33	  iter-145 as too expensive (~800–1500 LOC, presheaf-vs-sheaf bridge). Do not
34	  rebuild wholesale.
35	- **The chart-algebra pivot** (iter-144): built only the *converse* `df=0 ⟹
36	  constant` (KDM + `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`). It assumes `df=0`
37	  is supplied; nothing in tree produces it.
38	
39	## Key structural finding (read this first)
40	
41	`df = 0` is **irreducibly a global-sections statement** and factors *exactly* into
42	the project's two already-known deferred gaps:
43	
44	> `df ∈ H^0(C, Hom(f^*Ω_A, Ω_C))`.  With `f^*Ω_A ≅ O_C^{⊕g}` (gap i, the excised
45	> globalisation), this is `H^0(C, Ω_C^{⊕g}) = (H^0(C, Ω_C))^{⊕g}`.  So
46	> `df = 0  ⟺  H^0(C, Ω_C) = 0` (gap ii, Serre-duality-gated).
47	
48	There is **no chart-local route**: on an affine chart `V ⊆ C`, `Ω_C(V)` is *free
49	of rank 1* (nonzero), so the chart-by-chart KDM machinery the project already
50	built **cannot** see `df = 0`. The vanishing only appears after passing to global
51	sections. This is why the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot built the converse and
52	left `df=0` open: the two live on opposite sides of the local/global divide.
53	
54	Consequence: there is **no minimal Mathlib-idiomatic assembly** that yields `df=0`
55	from present in-tree material. The "lighter route" the directive hopes for does
56	not exist in `b80f227`. The cheap, portable analogue (linear-algebra glue, below)
57	only discharges the trivial implication once *both* gaps are filled.
58	
59	## Analogues found (ranked by porting cost)
60	
61	### Analogue: `Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top` / `LinearMap.ker_eq_top`
62	- **Citations**: `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Span.Basic` —
63	  `Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top`
64	  (`f = 0 ↔ ∀ s ∈ S, f s = 0` when `span S = ⊤`); also
65	  `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Ker.LinearMap.ker_eq_top` (`f.ker = ⊤ ↔ f = 0`),
66	  and `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Determinant.AlternatingMap.map_basis_eq_zero_iff`
67	  (`f ∘ basis = 0 ↔ f = 0`).
68	- **Domain**: linear algebra / module theory.
69	- **Same structural problem there**: a linear map out of a module *spanned by* a
70	  set is zero iff it kills the spanning set — exactly "morphism out of a
71	  generated/free object is determined by, and vanishes with, the generators".
72	- **Technique**: reduce `f = 0` to vanishing on a chosen spanning family (a basis
73	  of the free `O^{⊕g}`).
74	- **Mapping to project**: this is the *final glue step* — once `f^*Ω_A` is known
75	  globally free with a global basis `e_1,…,e_g` (gap i) and each `df(e_i) ∈
76	  H^0(C, Ω_C)` is shown zero (gap ii), `linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top`
77	  (applied to the global-section module, or to the sheaf-hom componentwise via
78	  `PresheafOfModules`/`SheafOfModules` `Hom` and `Scheme.Modules.Hom.zero_app`)
79	  concludes `df = 0`. ~5–20 LOC.
80	- **Porting cost**: **low**. But it is *not* the bottleneck — it presupposes both
81	  hard gaps already closed.
82	- **Verdict**: **ANALOGUE_FOUND** (glue only).
83	
84	### Analogue: `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra` (`addInvariantVectorField`, `mpullback_addInvariantVectorField`)
85	- **Citations**: `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra` —
86	  `addInvariantVectorField (v : AddGroupLieAlgebra I G) (g : G) : TangentSpace I g`
87	  (extends a tangent vector at the identity to a global vector field by
88	  left-translation); `mpullback_addInvariantVectorField`
89	  (`mpullback (g + ·) (addInvariantVectorField v) = addInvariantVectorField v` —
90	  translation invariance); `addInvariantVectorField_add` (additivity),
91	  `AddGroupLieAlgebra.instLieAlgebra`, `addInvariantVectorField_eq_mpullback`.
92	  Companion: `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Algebra.LeftInvariantDerivation`
93	  (`LeftInvariantDerivation.left_invariant`, `.toDerivation`, `.instLieAlgebra`).
94	- **Domain**: differential geometry / Lie theory (smooth manifolds).
95	- **Same structural problem there**: *the (co)tangent bundle of a group is globally
96	  trivialised by translating a single (co)vector at the identity* — Mathlib's
97	  manifold realisation of "`Ω_G` (resp. `TG`) is free of rank `dim G`", i.e.
98	  exactly gap (i).
99	- **Technique**: define the global frame `g ↦ (translation by g)_* v` from one
100	  identity vector `v`, prove invariance via `VectorField.mpullback` of the
101	  translation map (`mpullback_addInvariantVectorField`). This is **structurally
102	  identical** to the project's excised `shearMulRight`/`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`
103	  shear-translation globalisation.
104	- **Mapping to project**: it is the template the excised piece (i.b) was built
105	  against — `addInvariantVectorField` ↔ `mulRight`-globalised frame,
106	  `mpullback_addInvariantVectorField` ↔ `shearMulRight` invariance,
107	  `instLieAlgebra` ↔ `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`. **But** Mathlib's version lives
108	  entirely on `ChartedSpace`/`TangentSpace`/`VectorField.mpullback`/`LieAddGroup`
109	  manifold infrastructure with **no scheme-side counterpart**. Porting = rebuilding
110	  the excised presheaf globalisation against scheme cotangent presheaves.
111	- **Porting cost**: **high** — and the technique reduces to the directive's first
112	  Failed-approaches bullet.
113	- **Verdict**: **PARTIAL_ANALOGUE** (confirms the canonical shape and is the right
114	  design template *if* globalisation is ever rebuilt; not directly portable, and
115	  porting = the excised approach).
116	
117	### Analogue: `Module.rankAtStalk_eq_zero_iff_subsingleton`
118	- **Citation**: `Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.FreeLocus` —
119	  `Module.rankAtStalk M = 0 ↔ Subsingleton M` (`[Module.Flat] [Module.Finite]`).
120	- **Domain**: commutative algebra / quasi-coherent sheaf bookkeeping.
121	- **Same structural problem there**: detecting vanishing of a finite flat module by
122	  its stalk rank.
123	- **Mapping to project**: peripheral — useful only inside the gap-(i) trivialisation
124	  bookkeeping (ranks of `f^*Ω_A`), not for the global `df=0` vanishing. Does not
125	  produce `H^0 = 0`.
126	- **Porting cost**: low-medium, but not load-bearing.
127	- **Verdict**: **PARTIAL_ANALOGUE**.
128	
129	## Resolved-to-"Mathlib has nothing — must build in-tree / defer"
130	
131	These are the load-bearing pieces, and they are **absent**:
132	
133	- **Q1 (group-scheme cotangent triviality in algebraic geometry)**: ABSENT. The
134	  only Mathlib idiom for "(co)tangent of a group is trivial / translation-invariant"
135	  is the smooth-manifold `GroupLieAlgebra` / `LeftInvariantDerivation` stack above,
136	  which does not port (no scheme tangent-bundle API). `loogle "GrpObj,
137	  KaehlerDifferential"` → no results. Confirms `cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`
138	  (iter-126) Decision (i): NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL, ~800–1500 LOC if rebuilt.
139	- **Q2 (`H^0(C, Ω_C) = 0` from genus 0 / `H^1(C,O_C)=0`)**: ABSENT, and *this is
140	  the true bottleneck*. The connection `dim H^0(Ω) = dim H^1(O)` **is** Serre
141	  duality. `leansearch` for negative-degree / canonical-sheaf global-section
142	  vanishing, Riemann–Roch, dualizing sheaf → nothing (only `Proj` structure-sheaf
143	  plumbing). Reaffirms `serre-duality.md` (iter-110): no Serre duality, no
144	  dualizing/canonical sheaf, no trace morphism, no Riemann–Roch in `b80f227`;
145	  honest closure ~3000–8000 LOC. The project's own cohomology stack
146	  (`Cohomology/*`, `genus`) computes `H^1(O_C)` but has **no `H^0`-vanishing
147	  lemma, no direct-sum lemma, and no genus↔Ω bridge** (verified by reading the
148	  stack). The genus-0 hypothesis (`H^1(O_C)=0`) does **not** yield `H^0(Ω_C)=0`
149	  without the duality bridge.
150	- **Q4 (scheme-level sidestep: rigidity of maps to abelian varieties / rational
151	  curves constant)**: ABSENT. `leansearch` returns only `Scheme.RationalMap`
152	  (generic rational maps), `FormallyUnramified`, and affine-morphism plumbing —
153	  no abelian-variety rigidity, no "rational curve on AV is constant", no Mumford
154	  Rigidity Lemma, no `morphism from proper geom-connected to affine is constant`
155	  packaged. The classical proof of "genus-0 curve → AV is constant" itself routes
156	  through `H^0(Ω_C)=0` (or Albanese, also absent), so no sidestep removes the
157	  Q2 dependence.
158	
159	## Top suggestion
160	
161	There is **no cheap production of `df=0`**. The honest, load-bearing conclusion is
162	structural, not a lemma to port: `df=0` decomposes precisely into the two gaps the
163	project has *already* deferred — (i) the excised global cotangent triviality of `A`
164	(`~800–1500 LOC`), and (ii) `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0`, which is Serre duality on a curve
165	(`serre-duality.md`, `~3000–8000 LOC`, indefinitely deferred). The chart-by-chart
166	KDM machinery cannot reach `df=0` because the vanishing is global. The only
167	genuinely portable, cheap analogue (`Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top`)
168	is the trivial final glue and is useless until both gaps close.
169	
170	**Recommendation to the planner**: do **not** open a prover lane to "produce
171	`df=0`". Either (a) keep `rigidity_over_kbar` (`RigidityKbar.lean:75`) as a
172	**named-gap `sorry`** in the same family as `serre_duality_genus` and
173	`JacobianWitness` — the disposition `serre-duality.md` and `p1-hedge-...md` already
174	recommend — and route the blueprint to disclose `df=0` as gated on
175	{excised globalisation} + {Serre-duality `H^0(Ω)=0`}; or (b) if the project insists
176	on closing it, scope honestly for *both* multi-thousand-LOC builds, not a "minimal
177	assembly". When/if globalisation (gap i) is rebuilt, use
178	`Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra` as the *design template* (frame from
179	identity + translation-invariance), not as an importable dependency.
180	
181	## Verdict summary
182	
183	| Analogue | Domain | Porting cost | Verdict |
184	|---|---|---|---|
185	| `Submodule.linearMap_eq_zero_iff_of_span_eq_top` / `LinearMap.ker_eq_top` | linear algebra | low | ANALOGUE_FOUND (glue only) |
186	| `Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra` (`addInvariantVectorField`, `mpullback_…`) | diff. geometry / Lie | high (= excised approach) | PARTIAL_ANALOGUE |
187	| `Module.rankAtStalk_eq_zero_iff_subsingleton` | comm. algebra | low-med | PARTIAL_ANALOGUE |
188	| `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` from genus 0 (Serre duality) | alg. geometry | — | NO_USEFUL_ANALOGUE (absent / deferred) |
189	| group-scheme `Ω` triviality in alg. geometry (Q1) | alg. geometry | — | NO_USEFUL_ANALOGUE (absent) |
190	| AV rigidity / rational-curve-constant (Q4 sidestep) | alg. geometry | — | NO_USEFUL_ANALOGUE (absent) |
191	
192	**Overall**: `df=0` is a global-sections fact equal to {excised gap i} + {Serre-duality
193	gap ii}; Mathlib offers only the trivial linear-algebra glue and a non-portable
194	smooth-manifold template — keep `rigidity_over_kbar` a named gap.
195

The prior analogist (`df-zero-production-iter155`) already assessed route (a)/(b) feasibility thoroughly — re-dispatching it would be hollow. But one pivotal fact determines the whole route decision and I must verify it directly: **how is `genus` defined in this project?** If `genus := h⁰(Ω_C)`, route (a)'s Serre-duality gap is trivial for genus 0; if `genus := h¹(O_C)`, it needs the duality bridge.

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry
46

`genus C := dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` — the arithmetic genus. So route (a)'s gap (ii) `H⁰(Ω_C)=0` genuinely requires the Serre-duality bridge `h⁰(Ω)=h¹(O)`; it is NOT trivial. This confirms the analogist's finding and sharpens the decision. Let me check what's protected and the current Route A shape.

320:    \item[\textbf{(A.1)}] \emph{The relative Picard functor.} For a $k$-scheme $T$, write $C_T := C \times_k T$ and let $\pi \colon C_T \to T$ be the projection. The relative Picard functor is
327:    \item[\textbf{(A.2)}] \emph{Representability.} For $C$ smooth proper geometrically connected over $k$ with a $k$-rational point, $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ (after \'etale sheafification, if $k$ is not algebraically closed) is representable by a $k$-group scheme $\Pic_{C/k}$, locally of finite type. This is FGA's main representability theorem (Grothendieck, FGA~232); the smooth-proper-geometrically-connected hypothesis ensures that the cohomology-and-base-change machinery applies and that $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = k$, which is needed for the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ to be \'etale-locally injective.
329:    \item[\textbf{(A.3)}] \emph{The identity component.} Let $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ be the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$. As the identity component of a $k$-group scheme locally of finite type, $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is geometrically irreducible; for $C$ smooth proper of genus $g$, it is moreover a smooth proper $k$-group scheme of dimension $g$. Smoothness comes from the deformation-theoretic identification of its tangent space at the identity with $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ and homogeneity of the group action; properness from the fact that the natural map $\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}$ is an open and closed immersion onto a proper component (a consequence of the cohomological criterion: $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is the kernel of the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$, which is locally constant). Take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$.
331:    \item[\textbf{(A.4)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi universal morphism.} For each $P \in C(k)$, the Abel--Jacobi morphism
339:  \emph{Mathlib status for Route A.} Each sub-step is missing.
341:    \item Sub-step A.1 requires a working theory of relative line bundles on a product $C \times_k T$ and the descent of the abelian-group structure on $\Pic$ along $\pi^*$. The pieces are present individually but not assembled.
342:    \item Sub-step A.2 requires FGA representability for $\Pic_{C/k}$, which in turn requires Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure not in Mathlib (\texttt{HilbertScheme}, \texttt{QuotScheme}, and the cohomology-and-base-change for proper morphisms with finitely presented kernels). The construction engine for this Quot/Hilbert layer is Nitsure, ``Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes'' (FGA Explained; arXiv:math/0504590), \S\,4 (generic flatness and the flattening stratification) and \S\,5 (the construction of $\mathrm{Quot}$, hence $\mathrm{Hilb}$, as a scheme); see \texttt{references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.md}.
343:    \item Sub-step A.3 requires connectedness machinery for group schemes locally of finite type, the identity-component construction $G^0 \subseteq G$, and the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$.
344:    \item Sub-step A.4 requires the universal property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, which is the classical Albanese functoriality and is not in Mathlib in this form.
348:  \textbf{Iter-144 disposition.} Route B is a historical alternative \emph{not pursued} by the project. It is preserved here for scholarly context only. Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA --- is the committed M3 route per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition. The decomposition, gating Mathlib pieces, and midpoint LOC figure ($\sim 9000$ LOC) recorded below are the iter-123 audit values (\texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}); the chapter does not re-cost Route B under current Mathlib snapshots.
366:    presents the second arrow as a finite morphism whose source is the intermediate scheme. Since the fibres of $\sigma$ are connected, this intermediate scheme equals $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ scheme-theoretically (the Stein factorisation is trivial on the second arrow when fibres are connected, by Stein's classical theorem). The middle scheme is therefore $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ itself, which is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of dimension $g$. Translating by $-g P_0$ yields $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, and we take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as in Route A. The universal property is then established as in Sub-step A.4.
435:  Per the iter-144 M3 disposition (STRATEGY.md \S~M3), the project commits to Route A for the positive-genus arm and treats Route B as a historical alternative not pursued. The Mathlib build-outs gating the two surviving arms are therefore:
437:    \item[\textbf{($\alpha$)}] \emph{Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure plus FGA representability (COMMITTED M3 infrastructure --- iter-144 disposition).} This is the committed Mathlib build-out that unlocks Route A, which in turn discharges the positive-genus arm of the witness existence (\cref{def:positiveGenusWitness}). The single highest-cost build-out, since Hilbert/Quot schemes are upstream of much of modern algebraic geometry; but once present, the rest of Route A (identity component, smoothness, properness, universal property) follows by classical arguments already widely formalised in adjacent settings.
438:    \item[\textbf{($\gamma$)}] \emph{The rigidity theorem for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$.} This is the prerequisite of the genus-$0$ sub-case under Route A. The project packages it as \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), whose base field now carries $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$, gated on the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}: cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$. The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence is packaged as \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}), which over a general base field $k$ \emph{descends} the over-$\bar k$ rigidity conclusion to $k$: for each $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ it base-changes the data $(C, A, f, P)$ along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (genus is stable under base change for a geometrically irreducible curve, so $C_{\bar k}$ is again a genus-$0$ curve), applies \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} over $\bar k$ to conclude $f_{\bar k}$ is constant, then descends the morphism equality back to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f, via \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}). The $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch is vacuously true (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$). Independently of ($\alpha$), establishing ($\gamma$) together with the short descent step lets the genus-$0$ part of the witness be discharged unconditionally via \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}; the higher-genus part is packaged as \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness}, iter-134 scaffold) whose body closure is gated on Route A ($\alpha$). Per the iter-135 body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, the witness existence is now a case-split $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ delegating to \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} respectively, converting the formerly inline \texttt{sorry} into honest delegation to the two named scaffolds.
444:  \paragraph{Iter-135 body restructure.} The Lean body of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} is, per iter-135 refactor, a case-split $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ delegating to \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (genus-$0$ arm, \texttt{sorry} body closing iter-151+ under the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot --- see \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} body closure status, revised iter-145) and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} (positive-genus arm, \texttt{sorry} body closing M3 --- M3 is committed to Route A per the iter-144 disposition in STRATEGY.md \S~M3; sits behind M2 critical-path during the M2 wait window). The restructure converts the inline \texttt{sorry} on the witness-existence theorem itself into honest delegation to two named scaffolds; the witness existence is therefore conditionally established whenever both arms' bodies close (no Mathlib gap directly on the witness-existence theorem itself, only on each arm).
452:\begin{theorem}\leanok
503:The positive-genus sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} is packaged separately from the genus-$0$ arm so that the two arms can be closed independently. Whereas the genus-$0$ arm \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} routes through the rigidity declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, the positive-genus arm requires the full Albanese construction via Route A (Picard scheme via FGA --- Hilbert/Quot infrastructure plus FGA representability) of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, which is the committed M3 route per the iter-144 disposition in STRATEGY.md \S~M3. Route B (symmetric powers + Stein factorisation) is a historical alternative not pursued by the project.
505:\begin{theorem}\leanok
518:  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) \ge 1$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is the Albanese variety of $C$ (classically the identity component of the Picard scheme $\mathrm{Pic}^0_{C/k}$, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel--Jacobi morphism on $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$). The full $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}$ data (group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $g$, properness, geometric irreducibility, and Albanese universal property uniformly over $k$-rational marked points) is supplied by the Route A construction (per \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route A; Route B is documented as a historical alternative not pursued).
523:  This is the M3 arm of the witness existence. M3 is \textbf{committed to Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA} (per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition; $\sim 6500$ LOC midpoint per the iter-123 audit \texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}). The autonomous loop writes the $\sim 6500$-LOC Mathlib material directly in-tree; upstream PR extraction is an OPTIONAL downstream lift, not a project deliverable. The committed route produces the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness} in positive genus via the following Mathlib-prerequisite path:
525:    \item \emph{Route A: Hilbert/Quot infrastructure plus FGA representability of $\Pic_{C/k}$.} See \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route A for the four-step decomposition (A.1 relative Picard functor; A.2 representability; A.3 identity component; A.4 Abel--Jacobi universal morphism). The smallest entry point into Route A is the \texttt{RelativeSpec} functor (A.1 prerequisite; $\sim 700$--$1100$ LOC) per the iter-123 audit.
529:  M3 sits behind M2 critical-path during the M2 wait window; whether to start Route A scaffolding (smallest entry: \texttt{RelativeSpec} functor at $\sim 700$--$1100$ LOC) in parallel with M2 or strictly after M2 closes is a planner-level scheduling call. Existing iter-150 mathlib-analogist scheduling trigger: dispatched if cumulative M2.body-pile LOC exceeds $925$ LOC without piece (i.b) closing, OR if M2.a body closure has not landed by iter-160.
531:  The scaffold \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness} (landed iter-134) is load-bearing iter-135 onward for the genus-stratified body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (iter-135 refactor): the body case-splits $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ and delegates to \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} on the $\neg(\genus\,C = 0)$ arm via \texttt{Nat.pos\_of\_ne\_zero}. The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness} is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is contingent on Route A of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} being prosecuted in full.

`genus` is protected (so route (a)'s Serre-duality gap can't be dodged by redefinition), and the blueprint confirms Route A (the Pic⁰/FGA engine) is **mandatory for the positive-genus arm regardless of the genus-0 route choice**. This is the decisive fact: since Route A must be built anyway, routing genus-0 rigidity through it (route b, via `Alb(ℙ¹)=0`) amortises against mandatory work, whereas route (a) adds a standalone Serre-duality build (~3000–8000 LOC) on top.

I'm committing to **route (b)**. Let me rewrite STRATEGY.md to reflect this, then dispatch the critics to challenge it.

input.content:
# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
nine protected declarations, headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — the existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
object uniform over the $k$-rational pointing of a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis on the protected
signature. End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms.

The protected signature quantifies over arbitrary `C` with no $C(k) \neq \emptyset$
assumption. The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. The spine is
**pointed vs. unpointed**, not genus-0 vs. positive. `genus C := dim_k H^1(C,O_C)`
(arithmetic genus; protected).

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Route A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine** (now CRITICAL-PATH, serves BOTH arms) | committed (route-b decision), barely scaffolded | many (~40–70) | ~5100+ · 0/it | Hilbert/Quot representability (Nitsure §4–5); FGA `Pic_{C/k}` repr; identity component `Pic⁰`; Albanese universal property | A.2 representability is the riskiest, least-Mathlib-supported piece; smallest entry `RelativeSpec` ~700–1100 LOC. Project-fatal if it stalls — but mandatory regardless (positive-genus object) |
| **genus-0 rigidity via Route A** (`rigidity_over_kbar` re-proved through `Pic⁰`/Albanese) | gated on Route A engine | 2–4 | ~500–2000 · gated | `Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0` / `Alb(genus-0)=Spec k̄`; Albanese factorization for `f : C → A` killing `P` | small increment ONCE the engine exists; replaces the refuted differential `df=0` route |
| `genusZeroWitness` body + terminal cluster on `Spec k` + **`k̄→k` descent** | gated | 3–5 | 350–850 · gated | terminal-object cluster on `Spec k`; **faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality** along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` | descent (right-cancellation direction unconfirmed); skeleton already built iter-155 (6/7 fields closed, only `key` rigidity equation open) |
| **char-`p` genus-0 rigidity** (protected goal over arbitrary `[Field k]`, no `CharZero`) | gated, required, UNSCOPED | ? | ? · 0 | descent of constancy through Frobenius; OR a char-free Albanese argument | the differential route carried `[CharZero k]`; route-(b) Albanese argument MAY be char-free (Pic⁰ works in all char) — re-assess once the engine exists. No concrete plan yet |
| `nonempty_jacobianWitness` genus-stratified body | gated | 1 | <50 · gated | `by_cases h : genus C = 0` | trivial once both arms close |

## Routes

Single committed route for the witness OBJECT and BOTH universal-property arms:
**Route A (Picard scheme via FGA)**. One fallback (route a) is kept live with an
explicit reversal signal.

### Route A (COMMITTED, both arms) — Picard scheme / Albanese via FGA

Hilbert/Quot representability + identity-component `Pic⁰` + Albanese universal
property. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A". Literature in-tree:
`references/kleiman-picard.pdf` (§4 existence, §5 `Pic⁰`), `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`
(Quot/Hilbert engine).

**Positive-genus arm:** `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}`, a dim-`g` abelian variety; the Albanese
UP gives `isAlbaneseFor`. **Genus-0 arm:** `Alb(C_k̄) = Pic⁰(genus-0) = Spec k̄`
(`Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0`), so any `f : C → A` killing `P` factors through `Spec k̄` ⟹
constant — this is `rigidity_over_kbar` re-proved through the engine, NOT through
differentials. Both arms therefore share ONE engine.

**The route-(b) decision (iter-156).** The keystone `df = 0` for the differential
route is irreducibly a **global-sections** fact: `df = 0 ⟺ H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` given
`Ω_A` triviality (analogist `df-zero-production-iter155`). Since `genus := h^1(O_C)`
(protected), genus-0 does NOT give `h^0(Ω_C)=0` without **Serre duality**
(`h^0(Ω)=h^1(O)`, ~3000–8000 LOC, absent from Mathlib). Producing `df=0` thus needs
{Serre duality + `Ω_A` globalisation}. Because Route A's `Pic⁰` engine is
**mandatory anyway** for the positive-genus object, routing genus-0 rigidity through
it (`Alb(ℙ¹)=0`) amortises against required work; the differential route would add
a standalone Serre-duality build on top. **Route A wins on total budget.**

**Cost displaced downstream (unchanged by the route choice):** rigidity proved over
`k̄` means `genusZeroWitness` over arbitrary `k` must descend the constancy
conclusion along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` — faithfully-flat descent of a morphism equality
(`f_{k̄} = g_{k̄} ⟹ f = g`), NOT the right-cancellation `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`
gives. Assess against Mathlib's scheme-descent API when `genusZeroWitness` activates.

### Fallback (route a) — differential route via Serre duality

`df = 0` via {`Ω_A` global cotangent-triviality (~800–1500) + Serre duality
`H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (~3000–8000)}, REUSING the **closed, axiom-clean chart-algebra
envelope** (`mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (KDM), `constants_integral_over_base_field`,
`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`, thin `ext_of_diff_zero`) which supplies
the converse `df=0 ⟹ constant`. **Kept in tree, off the critical path** — NOT
deleted. **Reversal signal:** if Route A's A.2 representability stalls beyond ~10
iters of genuine effort, revisit route (a) for the genus-0 arm only (the chart
envelope is still available, and Serre duality decouples genus-0 from FGA risk).

## Open strategic questions

- **char-`p` genus-0 rigidity:** does the route-(b) Albanese argument close char-`p`
  for free (`Pic⁰` is char-agnostic), removing the need for the Frobenius-descent
  plan the differential route required? Re-assess once the engine exists. This is the
  most likely place the protected `[Field k]` goal silently fails.
- **Route A scheduling:** start scaffolding at the smallest entry (`RelativeSpec`
  functor, ~700–1100 LOC) immediately, or flesh the A.1–A.4 blueprint to
  prover-ready detail first? (Blueprint is currently a sketch — HARD GATE blocks a
  prover until a Route A chapter is complete+correct.)
- **`Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0` cost:** is computing `Pic(ℙ¹)=ℤ` / identity-component `= 0` a small
  increment on the engine, or does it need its own divisor-theory build?
- Should the chart-algebra envelope + GrpObj cotangent material be split out of the
  ~2600-line `RigidityKbar.tex` into a dedicated off-path chapter (structural job)?

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (CRITICAL-PATH, route (b)):**

- **Hilbert / Quot scheme representability** (Nitsure §4–5) + **FGA `Pic_{C/k}`
  representability** — the single highest-cost build, upstream of `Pic⁰`. ~3775 LOC.
- **identity-component subgroup scheme** `Pic⁰ ⊆ Pic`; degree map `Pic → ℤ`. ~800.
- **Albanese universal property** of `Pic⁰` (`isAlbaneseFor`): every `f : C → A`
  killing `P` factors uniquely through `Pic⁰` — goal-required for BOTH arms
  (genus-0 via `Alb=0`, positive-genus via the full UP); NOT yet itemised — true
  budget above ~5100.
- **`Pic⁰(genus-0)=0`** (`Pic(ℙ¹)=ℤ`): the genus-0 rigidity keystone under route (b).
- **faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality** along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` (in
  `genusZeroWitness`): injectivity-of-restriction, not right-cancellation.

**Gaps to fill (FALLBACK route a only — off critical path):**

- `df = 0` production: `Ω_A` global cotangent-triviality (~800–1500;
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`/`shearMulRight` design templates) + **Serre duality**
  `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (~3000–8000; no Mathlib dualizing sheaf / Riemann–Roch / genus↔Ω
  bridge). The chart `Ω_C(V)` is free rank-1, so the KDM stack cannot detect `df=0`.

**New project material introduced (in tree):**

- `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` — chart-algebra envelope (KDM, constants,
  `df_zero_factors`, thin `ext_of_diff_zero`). CLOSED, axiom-clean. Now route-(a)
  fallback asset, off critical path.
- `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` trio; `shearMulRight`. Design
  template for the `Ω_A` globalisation (fallback only).
- `Rigidity.lean` `ext_of_eqOnOpen` — dominant-source / separated-target rigidity
  packaging; consumed by the scheme-lift + Albanese uniqueness.

**Soundness rules (operational):** No new axioms (every closed decl `lean_verify`s
to `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`). A `sorry` must be the body of a
top-level named declaration — never buried in a `letI`/`have`/anonymous-`fun`
inside another decl (a buried `sorry` propagates a tainted value through `simp`
consumers without surfacing a `declaration uses sorry` warning).

`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`, thin `ext_of_diff_zero`) which supplies
the converse `df=0 ⟹ constant`. **Kept in tree, off the critical path** — NOT
deleted. **Reversal signal:** if Route A's A.2 representability stalls beyond ~10
iters of genuine effort, revisit route (a) for the genus-0 arm only (the chart
envelope is still available, and Serre duality decouples genus-0 from FGA risk).

## Open strategic questions

- **char-`p` genus-0 rigidity:** does the route-(b) Albanese argument close char-`p`
  for free (`Pic⁰` is char-agnostic), removing the need for the Frobenius-descent
  plan the differential route required? Re-assess once the engine exists. This is the
  most likely place the protected `[Field k]` goal silently fails.
- **Route A scheduling:** start scaffolding at the smallest entry (`RelativeSpec`
  functor, ~700–1100 LOC) immediately, or flesh the A.1–A.4 blueprint to
  prover-ready detail first? (Blueprint is currently a sketch — HARD GATE blocks a
  prover until a Route A chapter is complete+correct.)
- **`Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0` cost:** is computing `Pic(ℙ¹)=ℤ` / identity-component `= 0` a small
  increment on the engine, or does it need its own divisor-theory build?
- Should the chart-algebra envelope + GrpObj cotangent material be split out of the
  ~2600-line `RigidityKbar.tex` into a dedicated off-path chapter (structural job)?

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (CRITICAL-PATH, route (b)):**

- **Hilbert / Quot scheme representability** (Nitsure §4–5) + **FGA `Pic_{C/k}`
  representability** — the single highest-cost build, upstream of `Pic⁰`. ~3775 LOC.
- **identity-component subgroup scheme** `Pic⁰ ⊆ Pic`; degree map `Pic → ℤ`. ~800.
- **Albanese universal property** of `Pic⁰` (`isAlbaneseFor`): every `f : C → A`
  killing `P` factors uniquely through `Pic⁰` — goal-required for BOTH arms
  (genus-0 via `Alb=0`, positive-genus via the full UP); NOT yet itemised — true
  budget above ~5100.
- **`Pic⁰(genus-0)=0`** (`Pic(ℙ¹)=ℤ`): the genus-0 rigidity keystone under route (b).
- **faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality** along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` (in
  `genusZeroWitness`): injectivity-of-restriction, not right-cancellation.

**Gaps to fill (FALLBACK route a only — off critical path):**

- `df = 0` production: `Ω_A` global cotangent-triviality (~800–1500;
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`/`shearMulRight` design templates) + **Serre duality**
  `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (~3000–8000; no Mathlib dualizing sheaf / Riemann–Roch / genus↔Ω
  bridge). The chart `Ω_C(V)` is free rank-1, so the KDM stack cannot detect `df=0`.

**New project material introduced (in tree):**

- `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` — chart-algebra envelope (KDM, constants,
  `df_zero_factors`, thin `ext_of_diff_zero`). CLOSED, axiom-clean. Now route-(a)
  fallback asset, off critical path.
- `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` trio; `shearMulRight`. Design
  template for the `Ω_A` globalisation (fallback only).
- `Rigidity.lean` `ext_of_eqOnOpen` — dominant-source / separated-target rigidity
  packaging; consumed by the scheme-lift + Albanese uniqueness.

**Soundness rules (operational):** No new axioms (every closed decl `lean_verify`s
to `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`). A `sorry` must be the body of a
top-level named declaration — never buried in a `letI`/`have`/anonymous-`fun`
inside another decl.
---

## References index

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `challenge.lean.ref` | Original AI challenge file by Christian Merten — formal statement of the missing definitions/theorems for the Jacobian of an algebraic curve. Signatures here are authoritative. |
| `stacks-varieties.md` → `.tex` | Stacks ch.33 "Varieties" — geom-reduced, smooth⇒geom regular/normal/reduced, `H^0(X,O)` lemma. |
| `stacks-fields.md` → `.tex` | Stacks ch.9 "Fields" — purely inseparable, sep-then-insep factorisation. |
| `stacks-algebra.md` → `.tex` | Stacks ch.10 "Commutative Algebra" — standard smooth ⇒ `Ω` free. |
| `stacks-coherent.md` → `.tex` | Stacks ch.30 "Cohomology of Schemes" — flat base change of `R^i f_*`. |
| `kleiman-picard.md` → `.pdf` | Kleiman, "The Picard scheme" (FGA Explained). Route A source. §4 existence, §5 `Pic⁰` (Jacobian, pp.36–51), §6 `Pic^τ` finiteness. |
| `nitsure-hilbert-quot.md` → `.pdf` | Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes" (FGA Explained). Quot/Hilbert construction engine behind Route A. |

## Blueprint summary

- `AbelJacobi.tex` — the Abel–Jacobi map; transits `nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex` — cotangent space at identity (piece (i)); `shearMulRight`.
- `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` — Mayer–Vietoris LES for sheaf cohomology (k-module coeffs).
- `Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex` — sheaf condition along the structure-sheaf forget composite.
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex` — structure sheaf as sheaf of abelian groups; sheafification, Ext.
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` — sheaves of k-modules; structure sheaf as k-module sheaf. Computes `H^1(O_C)`; NO `H^0`-vanishing / genus↔Ω bridge.
- `Differentials.tex` — relative cotangent presheaf; `smooth_locally_free_omega` (discloses the false-converse pitfall).
- `Genus.tex` — genus of a smooth proper curve; `genus := dim_k H^1(C,O_C)`.
- `Jacobian.tex` — the Jacobian as an abelian variety; `nonempty_jacobianWitness`, `genusZeroWitness`, `positiveGenusWitness`; Route A (A.1–A.4) + Route B (historical, not pursued).
- `Rigidity.tex` — scheme-level rigidity (`ext_of_eqOnOpen`); Mumford §4 abelian-variety case.
- `RigidityKbar.tex` — rigidity over k̄: genus-0 curve → group scheme constant; `rigidity_over_kbar` (NAMED GAP); chart-algebra envelope (KDM, constants, df_zero_factors); GrpObj cotangent-globalisation design-template blocks (off-path).

- iter-155: Route C "resolve the df=0 production question" — addressed (route-(b) decision made this iter).
- iter-155: Route A "itemise the g≥1 Albanese universal property" — addressed (now itemised under Mathlib gaps).

Coupling that statement to `Alb(C_k̄)=Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0` ties the genus-0 arm to the project's riskiest dependency (A.2 representability + the `Pic(ℙ¹)=ℤ` computation, itself flagged as an open cost) for no necessity. The planner must either (a) adopt/cost the targeted char-free `Mor(ℙ¹,A)`-constant rigidity lemma above as the genus-0 path (decoupled from representability), or (b) record an explicit rebuttal in `iter/iter-156/plan.md` explaining why genus-0 rigidity genuinely requires `Pic⁰` representability rather than just the rigidity lemma.
47	
48	- **Route A: CHALLENGE — drop or justify the "descent cost is unchanged by the route choice" claim.** The strategy asserts the `Spec k̄ → Spec k` faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality is route-independent. This is suspect: a `Pic⁰`-over-`k` engine yields `Alb(C)=Pic⁰(C)=Spec k` *directly over `k`* (even for a pointless conic, where `Pic⁰` is still trivial), apparently **avoiding** the descent; whereas the targeted-`ℙ¹` route needs `C_k̄≅ℙ¹` and therefore *does* incur descent. The descent direction is itself flagged "unconfirmed." The planner should not claim route-independence; the descent obligation differs across the genus-0 options and should be costed per-option.
49	
50	- **Format: DRIFTED — strip per-iter narrative.** Remove the `(iter-156)`, `iter-155`, and `df-zero-production-iter155` references from STRATEGY.md (move any needed history to `iter/iter-156/plan.md`); state the routing decision atemporally.
51	
52	## Overall verdict
53	
54	A fresh mathematician would accept that **for the positive-genus object the FGA `Pic⁰` engine is the right and essentially unavoidable construction**, and that abandoning the differential route's Serre-duality build for the genus-0 arm is defensible (Serre duality is `~3000–8000` LOC and carries a char-`p` gap; the chart envelope was always insufficient on its own, so route (b) discards no goal-closing work). The committing logic is **not** sunk-cost-blind in either direction. **However**, the central amortization argument is materially overstated: the genus-0 arm needs a *rigidity lemma*, not `Pic⁰` *representability*, so binding it to the project's riskiest, zero-velocity, "project-fatal" dependency is an unforced coupling. A targeted char-free "`Mor(ℙ¹,A)` is constant" lemma — reusing the rigidity machinery the positive-genus arm needs anyway, but not Quot/Hilbert representability — would close genus-0 earlier, char-freely, and independently of the highest-risk piece, and is the strongest insurance the strategy currently lacks. Resolve the two Route-A challenges and strip the iter narrative; then the strategy is sound to proceed.
55

The strategy-critic's correction is decisive and I'm adopting it: **genus-0 needs a rigidity *statement*, not Pic⁰ *representability*.** The witness object is `Spec k` (trivial). So genus-0 should be decoupled from the riskiest A.2 representability piece. The critic's route (c) — a targeted char-free "Mor(genus-0 curve, group scheme) constant" rigidity lemma — is the lead candidate, though its feasibility (does the classical rigidity-lemma proof genuinely avoid Albanese/differentials?) is a real open math question, since the iter-155 analogist found "AV rigidity" routes classically through H⁰(Ω)=0 OR Albanese.

# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean`):
nine protected declarations, headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — the existence of an Albanese / Jacobian
object uniform over the $k$-rational pointing of a smooth proper geometrically
irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis on the protected
signature. End-state: zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms.

The protected signature quantifies over arbitrary `C` with no $C(k) \neq \emptyset$
assumption. The witness OBJECT `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. The spine is
**pointed vs. unpointed**, not genus-0 vs. positive. `genus C := dim_k H^1(C,O_C)`
(arithmetic genus; protected — cannot be re-typed).

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Route A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine** (witness OBJECT for positive genus; CRITICAL PATH) | committed, blueprint sketch-level | many (~40–70) | ~5100+ · 0/it | Hilbert/Quot representability (Nitsure §4–5); FGA `Pic_{C/k}` repr; identity component `Pic⁰`; Albanese universal property | A.2 representability is the riskiest, least-Mathlib-supported piece; smallest entry `RelativeSpec` ~700–1100 LOC. Project-fatal if it stalls — but mandatory regardless (the positive-genus object is `Pic⁰`) |
| **genus-0 rigidity** (`rigidity_over_kbar`; object `J=Spec k` already trivial) | proof route under determination; DECOUPLED from representability | 2–6 | ~300–2000 · gated | a char-free "Mor(genus-0 curve, group scheme) is constant" rigidity lemma (Mumford rigidity lemma / theorem of the cube) — reuses the AV-theory/rigidity half, NOT Quot/Hilbert repr | needs only a rigidity STATEMENT, not Pic⁰ representability; lead route (c) feasibility (does the rigidity lemma give it without Albanese/differentials?) is an open math question pending the AV source |
| `genusZeroWitness` body + terminal cluster on `Spec k` + **`k̄→k` descent** | gated | 3–5 | 350–850 · gated | terminal-object cluster on `Spec k`; **faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality** along `Spec k̄ → Spec k` (route-dependent: a `Pic⁰`-over-`k` argument may avoid it; a `C_k̄≅ℙ¹` argument incurs it) | descent right-cancellation direction unconfirmed; skeleton already built (6/7 fields closed, only the `key` rigidity equation open) |
| **char-`p` genus-0 rigidity** (protected goal over arbitrary `[Field k]`, no `CharZero`) | gated; may dissolve under route (c) | ? | ? · 0 | none if the rigidity lemma is char-free (it is); a Frobenius-descent plan only if the differential fallback (a) is used | the differential fallback carries `[CharZero k]`; the rigidity-lemma route (c) is char-free, likely removing this gap entirely — confirm when route (c) is settled |
| `nonempty_jacobianWitness` genus-stratified body | gated | 1 | <50 · gated | `by_cases h : genus C = 0` | trivial once both arms close |

## Routes

The witness OBJECT for positive genus is built by **Route A (Picard scheme via
FGA)** — mandatory and essentially unavoidable. The genus-0 arm is a separate,
lower-risk rigidity statement (object trivial); its proof route is under
determination, with two fallbacks held live.

### Route A (COMMITTED) — Picard scheme / Albanese via FGA

Hilbert/Quot representability + identity-component `Pic⁰` + Albanese universal
property. Decomposition A.1–A.4 in `Jacobian.tex` § "Route A". Literature in-tree:
`references/kleiman-picard.pdf` (§4 existence, §5 `Pic⁰`), `references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.pdf`
(Quot/Hilbert engine).

**Positive-genus arm:** `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}`, a dim-`g` abelian variety; the Albanese
UP gives `isAlbaneseFor`. This is the project's critical path: the object must be a
real dim-`g` abelian variety even when `C(k)=∅`, so the FGA engine is required
unconditionally.

### Genus-0 rigidity — the keystone `rigidity_over_kbar`, decoupled

The genus-0 witness OBJECT is the trivial `J = Spec k` (skeleton already 6/7
closed); it needs **no** Picard scheme. What remains is a single rigidity
STATEMENT: over `k̄`, a genus-0 curve is `≅ ℙ¹`, and any pointed `f : C → A` killing
`P` is constant. Three candidate proof routes, ranked:

- **(c) [LEAD] targeted char-free AV-rigidity lemma.** "Every morphism from a
  genus-0 curve (`ℙ¹`) to a smooth proper group scheme is constant", via the
  rigidity lemma / theorem of the cube. Reuses the rigidity machinery the
  positive-genus Albanese UP needs anyway, but NOT Quot/Hilbert representability —
  so it is decoupled from the project's riskiest piece, and is char-free (no Serre
  duality, no Frobenius gap). **Feasibility open:** the classical "Mor(ℙ¹,A)
  constant" is usually proved through `H^0(Ω)=0` or the Albanese; whether the bare
  rigidity lemma yields it without either is the question to settle from the AV
  source (Mumford/Milne).
- **(b) byproduct of the engine.** Once Route A exists, `Alb(genus-0)=Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0`,
  so `f` factors through `Spec k̄`. Free, but couples genus-0 to A.2 representability
  — only attractive if (c) proves infeasible.
- **(a) fallback — differential route via Serre duality.** `df=0` via {`Ω_A`
  cotangent-triviality (~800–1500) + Serre duality `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (~3000–8000)},
  REUSING the closed, axiom-clean chart-algebra envelope (KDM,
  `constants_integral_over_base_field`, `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`)
  which supplies the converse `df=0 ⟹ constant`. **Kept in tree, off path** — not
  deleted. Carries a char-`p` gap. Reversal signal: if (c) is infeasible AND Route
  A's A.2 stalls, this decouples genus-0 from FGA risk.

## Open strategic questions

- **Route (c) feasibility:** does the rigidity lemma / theorem of the cube prove
  "Mor(genus-0 curve, group scheme) constant" WITHOUT the Albanese or differentials?
  Settle from the AV source. If yes, genus-0 closes early, char-free, decoupled from
  representability — the strongest insurance the project can hold.
- **Route A scheduling:** flesh A.1–A.4 to prover-ready blocks (lead: `RelativeSpec`
  functor entry, ~700–1100 LOC) before scaffolding. Blueprint is currently a sketch;
  the HARD GATE blocks a prover until a Route A chapter is complete+correct.
- **`k̄→k` descent is route-dependent**, not a fixed cost: a `Pic⁰`-over-`k` argument
  may produce `Alb(C)=Spec k` directly (no descent); a `C_k̄≅ℙ¹` argument incurs the
  faithfully-flat descent of a morphism equality (direction unconfirmed). Cost it
  per genus-0 route once chosen.
- Should the chart-algebra envelope + GrpObj cotangent material be split out of the
  large `RigidityKbar.tex` into a dedicated off-path chapter (structural job)?

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (CRITICAL PATH — Route A engine, positive-genus object):**

- **Hilbert / Quot scheme representability** (Nitsure §4–5) + **FGA `Pic_{C/k}`
  representability** — the single highest-cost build, upstream of `Pic⁰`. ~3775 LOC.
- **identity-component subgroup scheme** `Pic⁰ ⊆ Pic`; degree map `Pic → ℤ`. ~800.
- **Albanese universal property** of `Pic⁰` (`isAlbaneseFor` for `g ≥ 1`): every
  `f : C → A` killing `P` factors uniquely through `Pic⁰`. Goal-required; uses the
  rigidity lemma; NOT yet itemised in ~5100 — true budget higher.

**Gaps to fill (genus-0 rigidity, route (c) lead):**

- **char-free AV-rigidity lemma** "Mor(genus-0 curve, smooth proper group scheme) is
  constant" via the rigidity lemma / theorem of the cube. Shares machinery with the
  Albanese UP; absent from Mathlib (no AV rigidity, no "rational curve on AV
  constant"). Source to bundle: Mumford/Milne, Abelian Varieties.

**Gaps to fill (FALLBACK route (a) only — off critical path):**

- `df = 0` production: `Ω_A` global cotangent-triviality (~800–1500;
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`/`shearMulRight` design templates) + **Serre duality**
  `H^0(C,Ω_C)=0` (~3000–8000; no Mathlib dualizing sheaf / Riemann–Roch / genus↔Ω
  bridge). The chart `Ω_C(V)` is free rank-1, so the KDM stack cannot detect `df=0`.

**New project material introduced (in tree):**

- `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` — chart-algebra envelope (KDM, constants,
  `df_zero_factors`, thin `ext_of_diff_zero`). CLOSED, axiom-clean. Route-(a)
  fallback asset, off critical path.
- `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` trio; `shearMulRight`. Design
  template for the `Ω_A` globalisation (fallback only).
- `Rigidity.lean` `ext_of_eqOnOpen` — dominant-source / separated-target rigidity
  packaging; consumed by the scheme-lift + Albanese uniqueness.

**Soundness rules (operational):** No new axioms (every closed decl `lean_verify`s
to `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`). A `sorry` must be the body of a
top-level named declaration — never buried in a `letI`/`have`/anonymous-`fun`
inside another decl.

# Reference Retriever Directive

## Slug
abelian-varieties

## What I need

The project needs the canonical source for **abelian-variety rigidity**, to settle
two questions:

1. **The rigidity lemma** (Mumford's "rigidity lemma" / via the theorem of the cube)
   and its corollary that **every morphism from `ℙ¹` (a genus-0 curve) to an abelian
   variety / smooth proper group scheme is constant** — equivalently, an abelian
   variety contains no rational curves. I need the verbatim statement AND proof so I
   can determine whether this "Mor(ℙ¹,A) constant" fact can be proved via the bare
   rigidity lemma WITHOUT invoking Serre duality (`H^0(Ω)=0`) or the Albanese/Picard
   construction.
2. The **Albanese universal property** of `Pic⁰` for a curve (Abel–Jacobi
   functoriality), to back the project's Route A positive-genus arm.

## Sources to fetch (in priority order)

1. **J.S. Milne, "Abelian Varieties" (course notes)** — freely available at
   `https://www.jmilne.org/math/CourseNotes/av.html` (PDF link on that page, typically
   `https://www.jmilne.org/math/CourseNotes/AV.pdf`). This is open-access and contains
   the rigidity theorem, "morphisms from `ℙ¹` to an abelian variety are constant", and
   the Albanese material. PRIMARY target — please fetch this.
2. **Mumford, "Abelian Varieties"** (Tata Institute / Oxford, 1970) — the canonical
   source; §4 "The Rigidity Lemma". Likely paywalled / no open copy. Best-effort only;
   if not openly available, report "not found" for this one and rely on Milne.

If Milne's AV notes are retrieved, that is sufficient for my needs. Download the real
PDF (verify it is the document, not an HTML error page), write the pointer
`references/abelian-varieties.md` with a section/contents map (note the page/section
numbers of: the rigidity lemma; the "Mor(ℙ¹,A) constant" / no-rational-curves
corollary; the Albanese universal property), and register it in `references/summary.md`.
